name: Fiesta de la Vendimia
description: Argentina's wine harvest festival with grape stomping, concerts and the crowning of the harvest queen.

name: Carnaval de Montevideo
description: The world's longest carnival lasting the entire month with candombe drums and murga satirical groups.

name: Inti Raymi
description: Inca Festival of the Sun reenacted at Sacsayhuaman fortress with 500 costumed performers.

name: Semana Santa Sevilla
description: The world's most spectacular Holy Week with hundreds of hooded penitents carrying massive floats through ancient streets.

name: Las Fallas
description: Massive satirical papier-mache sculptures burnt in spectacular fires across Valencia on the final night.

name: Semana Grande
description: Bilbao's biggest week with Basque folk culture, concerts and the famous white handkerchief ritual at midnight.

name: Sinterklaas Festival
description: Dutch winter tradition where Saint Nicholas arrives by steamboat from Spain bringing gifts for children.

name: Koningsdag
description: King's birthday celebration where Amsterdam turns completely orange with boat parties and street markets.

name: Midsommar
description: Sweden's midsummer celebration with maypole dancing, flower crowns and herring feasts in the longest day.

name: Aurora Festival
description: World's northernmost arts festival celebrating the Northern Lights with concerts, films and ice music.

name: St. Patrick's Day
description: Ireland's national day with the world's most authentic St. Patrick's celebration and massive street parades.

name: Rose of Tralee
description: Ireland's beloved international festival celebrating Irish women worldwide with pageantry and traditional music.

name: Carnaval de Cadiz
description: Spain's wittiest carnival where satirical singing groups called chirigotas mock politicians in the streets.

name: Palio di Siena
description: Medieval bareback horse race around Siena's Piazza del Campo between rival city districts — raw and electric.

name: Infiorata di Spello
description: Tiny Umbrian town creates intricate flower petal carpets covering the entire street for the Corpus Christi procession.

name: Carnevale di Viareggio
description: Italy's most satirical carnival with giant mechanical floats mocking politicians and celebrities on the seafront.

name: Bastille Day
description: France's national day with the world's most spectacular military parade on the Champs-Elysees and fireworks over the Eiffel Tower.

name: Nice Carnival
description: Europe's biggest carnival with spectacular floats parading along the Promenade des Anglais for two weeks.

name: Fete de la Musique
description: Every amateur and professional musician plays free in the streets of France on the summer solstice — magical.

name: Zurich Street Parade
description: Europe's biggest techno parade with a million people dancing behind massive sound trucks along Lake Zurich.

name: Montreux Jazz Festival
description: One of the world's greatest music festivals beside Lake Geneva where Miles Davis and Ella Fitzgerald once played.

name: Carnival of Basel
description: Switzerland's most important carnival beginning with a total blackout at 4am as thousands of lanterns appear.

name: Wacken Open Air
description: World's biggest heavy metal festival in a tiny German village attracting 85,000 metalheads from 100 countries.

name: Cologne Carnival
description: Germany's most exuberant carnival with women cutting men's ties on Rose Monday and the massive street parade.

name: Drachenstich
description: Germany's oldest folk play where a giant mechanical dragon is slain by a knight in a 600-year-old tradition.

name: Ollesummer
description: Baltic's biggest music festival in Tallinn's medieval old town with Estonian and international acts.

name: Song and Dance Festival
description: UNESCO-listed choral festival where 30,000 singers perform together — the living symbol of Estonian identity.

name: Krakow Jewish Culture Festival
description: Europe's most important Jewish culture festival in Krakow's historic Kazimierz district with klezmer music.

name: Przystanek Woodstock
description: Poland's massive free music festival attracting half a million people along the Oder River each summer.

name: Rozhen Folk Festival
description: Bulgaria's biggest folklore festival in the Rhodope Mountains with bagpipe music and traditional costumes.

name: EXIT Festival
description: Europe's best music festival held in a 18th century fortress with multiple stages and 200,000 attendees.

name: Gucha Trumpet Festival
description: World's wildest brass band competition where Serbian and Romani trumpet players battle for supremacy in a tiny village.

name: Dubrovnik Summer Festival
description: Theatre, opera and dance performances in Dubrovnik's ancient fortresses and open-air venues.

name: Sinjska Alka
description: UNESCO-listed 18th century knight tournament where horsemen at full gallop aim lances at a small metal ring.

name: Kaustinen Folk Music Festival
description: World's northernmost major folk festival with Finnish fiddle music and Nordic folk dance traditions.

name: Juhannus Midsummer
description: Finnish midsummer with massive bonfires by lakes, sauna rituals and dancing all through the white night.

name: Aarhus Festival
description: Scandinavia's biggest cultural festival transforming Denmark's second city with art, music and theatre.

name: Roskilde Festival
description: Northern Europe's biggest music festival in the Viking city with 130,000 people and humanitarian ethos.

name: National Day Qatar
description: Qatar's national celebrations with spectacular fireworks, camel parades and traditional Ardah sword dances.

name: Dubai Shopping Festival
description: Month-long Dubai extravaganza with fireworks, global concerts, and massive discounts across the city.

name: Muscat Festival
description: Oman's biggest festival showcasing the country's unique Arab heritage with traditional arts, falconry and camel races.

name: Jerash Festival
description: Performing arts festival in the remarkably preserved Roman city of Jerash with classical and Arabic performances.

name: Nowruz in Tehran
description: Persian New Year celebrated for 13 days with haft-seen table, fire jumping and family reunions across Iran.

name: Turkmenbashi Nowruz
description: Central Asian spring festival with horse races, traditional wrestling and communal pilaf cooking in white marble city.

name: Navruz in Samarkand
description: Persian New Year in the ancient Silk Road city with sumalak wheat pudding and music in Registan Square.

name: Naadam Festival
description: Mongolia's national festival of three manly games — wrestling, archery and horse racing on the steppe.

name: Eagle Festival
description: Kazakh eagle hunters compete with their golden eagles in a stunning display of Central Asian nomadic culture.

name: Tsagaan Sar
description: Mongolian Lunar New Year with elaborate greeting rituals, traditional food and visiting relatives in gers.

name: Yak Festival
description: Remote festival celebrating the nomadic Layap people and their yaks in one of Bhutan's most inaccessible villages.

name: Punakha Drubchen
description: Bhutan's most spectacular festival reenacting a 17th century battle victory with warrior dancers in the ancient dzong.

name: Thimphu Tsechu
description: Bhutan's capital festival with sacred Cham mask dances and the unfurling of an enormous silk thangka at dawn.

name: Pahiyas Festival
description: Houses decorated with colorful kiping rice wafers and vegetables in the most visually stunning Filipino festival.

name: Sinulog Festival
description: Philippines' biggest religious street festival honoring Santo Nino with millions of dancers in elaborate costumes.

name: Ati-Atihan Festival
description: The mother of all Philippine festivals where participants paint faces black and dance wildly to beating drums.

name: Bun Festival Cheung Chau
description: Hong Kong island festival with bun towers, lion dances and a uniquely eerie parade of children carried on poles.

name: Dragon Boat Festival
description: Ancient festival with dragon boat races on the Pearl River honoring the poet Qu Yuan who drowned 2300 years ago.

name: Water Lantern Festival
description: Thousands of sky lanterns bearing wishes released simultaneously creating a breathtaking river of light.

name: Jeongwol Daeboreum
description: Korean full moon festival with bonfires, traditional folk games and eating five grain rice for good health.

name: Chuseok
description: Korean harvest thanksgiving with ancestral rites, traditional games and the making of crescent-shaped songpyeon rice cakes.

name: Tanabata Star Festival
description: Japan's star festival where paper wishes are hung on bamboo as massive colourful decorations fill Sendai's streets.

name: Kanda Matsuri
description: One of Tokyo's three great festivals with mikoshi portable shrines carried through Akihabara every two years.

name: Yuki Matsuri Asahikawa
description: Hokkaido's massive snow sculpture festival competing with Sapporo but with a more local and authentic atmosphere.

name: Aoi Matsuri
description: Kyoto's oldest festival with an 800-person procession in 8th century Heian court costume through the old city.

name: Lanten Ethnic Festival
description: Remote northern Laos festival celebrating the Lanten ethnic minority with paper-making, indigo dyeing and ritual songs.

name: That Luang Festival
description: Laos's biggest religious festival at the golden That Luang stupa with candlelit processions and traditional dances.

name: Holi in Phnom Penh
description: Cambodian Water Festival with boat races on the Tonle Sap river reversal and spectacular floating illuminations.

name: Tazaungdaing Festival
description: Myanmar's festival of lights with thousands of hot air balloons and elaborate woven lanterns illuminating the night.

name: Thingyan
description: Myanmar New Year water festival with water throwing, music and the religious bathing of Buddha images.

name: Esala Maha Perahera
description: Sri Lanka's most magnificent procession with 100 decorated elephants parading through Kandy's ancient streets.

name: Vel Festival
description: Hindu festival transporting the sacred Vel trident of Murugan in a massive silver chariot through Colombo.

name: Rara Lake Festival
description: Remote festival at Nepal's largest lake in Rara National Park celebrating the Thakuri people's spring traditions.

name: Yomari Punhi
description: Newari harvest festival where fig-shaped rice dumplings are offered to deities in an ancient medieval town.

name: Bisket Jatra
description: Newari New Year chariot festival where a massive chariot is pulled through Bhaktapur's medieval streets.

name: Maghe Sankranti
description: Sacred bathing festival at the confluence of Trishuli and Kali Gandaki rivers with thousands of pilgrims.

name: Timket
description: Ethiopian Epiphany with priests carrying the Ark of Covenant replicas through streets in dazzling processions.

name: Irreecha
description: Oromo people's thanksgiving festival at Lake Hora with millions dressed in white offering grass and flowers.

name: Gerewol Festival
description: Wodaabe nomad festival where men spend days beautifying themselves competing for female attention in the Sahara.

name: Ouidah Voodoo Festival
description: West Africa's biggest voodoo festival with animal sacrifices, trance rituals and fetish ceremonies on the beach.

name: Crop Over Festival
description: Barbados's vibrant harvest carnival with costumed revelers, calypso music and the ceremonial burning of Mr. Harding.

name: Trinidad Carnival
description: The original Caribbean carnival and mother of all soca festivals with intricate mas costumes and steelpan music.

name: Junkanoo
description: Bahamas's most important cultural event with elaborate costumes of crepe paper and cowbells parading at 2am.

name: Colourama
description: South Africa's own Holi celebration with music and colors adapted by the South African Indian community.

name: Klein Karoo National Arts Festival
description: Afrikaans arts festival in the ostrich capital of the world with theatre, music and visual art.

name: WOMAD Adelaide
description: World music arts and dance festival bringing musicians from every continent to Australia's festival city.

name: Tamworth Country Music Festival
description: Australia's biggest music festival and Southern Hemisphere's largest country music event in the New England region.

name: Kangaroo Island Racing Carnival
description: Quirky annual horse racing event on Australia's third largest island combining bush racing with wildlife watching.

name: Pasifika Festival
description: World's largest Pacific Island cultural festival celebrating the diverse Polynesian, Melanesian and Micronesian cultures.

name: Hokitika Wildfoods Festival
description: New Zealand's most adventurous food festival where locals eat huhu grubs, worms and other West Coast wild foods.

name: Polka Festival
description: America's National Polka Festival in a Czech immigrant town keeping Central European folk traditions alive.

name: Mermaid Parade
description: Coney Island's beloved quirky parade where 3000 costumed mermaids, sea creatures and revelers march on the boardwalk.

name: Telluride Bluegrass Festival
description: Colorado's mountain music festival in a stunning box canyon with world-class bluegrass and Americana artists.

name: Albuquerque Balloon Fiesta
description: World's biggest hot air balloon festival with 500 balloons filling New Mexico's blue skies each October morning.

name: New Orleans Jazz Festival
description: Legendary American music festival celebrating jazz, blues, gospel and Louisiana culture in its birthplace.

name: Dia de los Muertos LA
description: Los Angeles's massive Day of the Dead celebration in Hollywood Forever Cemetery with altars, music and processions.

name: Carnaval San Francisco
description: America's largest Latino carnival in the Mission District celebrating Caribbean and Latin American cultures.
